results.title: Riverine communities to benefit from K140m compensation package
results.description: Mining Minister Solen Loifa has outlined support for riverine communities affected by the Pogera Mine, confirming in Parliament that K140 million will be shared over seven years. He was responding to questions from Governor for Western Province and a supplementary question from Member for North Fly, Donald James, who asked last week: “Would the Minister consider including a social licence framework for riverine communities affected by the mine?” To access this post, you must purchase Web , Web – One-Day , Web – 14-Day , Web – One-Month , Web – Three-Month , Web – Six-Month , Web – Annual , Web & eBook , Web & eBook – One-Day , Web & eBook – 14-Day , Web & eBook – One-Month , Web & eBook – Three-Month , Web & eBook – Six-Month or Web & eBook – Annual . The post Riverine communities to benefit from K140m compensation package appeared first on Post Courier .

results.title: Cliff-edge warning: Fewer than 10 per cent of Brits to achieve a comfortable retirement
results.description: Large numbers of Brits are at risk of facing a retirement ‘cliff edge’ as people fail to save enough to achieve a moderate or comfortable retirement. For someone living on their own, a comfortable lifestyle would require an annual income of £45,400, a level only nine per cent of Brits are on track to hit, [...]

results.title: AI Jitters Fuel Biggest Swings in India’s IT Stocks Since 2020
Most US companies continue to operate in Russia - AmCham chief
results.description: American businesses have been operating in Russia for many years, some big brands have left, but most U.S. companies continue to operate in Russia, AmCham Russia President and CEO Robert Agee said on the sidelines of the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum.
